message:
fix(http): prevent stale keep-alive probe failures by using ephemeral probe clients and increasing idle timeout

Fix false liveness probe failures caused by the supervisor reusing a
pooled HTTP connection that the server had already closed. The shared
probe client's 1-second idle timeout was shorter than the 2-second probe
interval, causing stale keep-alive connections to accumulate.

- Increase HTTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT from 1s to 15s so server-side keep-alive
  outlives the supervisor probe cadence and normal MCP request bursts
- Increase HTTP_SUPERVISOR_PROBE_TIMEOUT from 300ms to 1s for more
  realistic probe timing under load
- Replace shared long-lived probe HttpClient with per-probe ephemeral
  client (idle_timeout=ZERO) to guarantee each probe uses a fresh
  connection and cannot inherit stale state
- Add HttpHealthProbeFailure enum (Timeout, Status, Transport) with
  elapsed_ms for structured diagnostic reporting
- Log probe_failure details on liveness check failure for debugging
- Add assertion test: HTTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT > HTTP_SUPERVISOR_PROBE_INTERVAL
- Update io_uring reactor preference comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
